name: Biparietal diameter at 28 weeks
tier_rationale: Tier A from Buck Louis 2015 NICHD Fetal Growth Studies (n=2334 low-risk singletons; longitudinal ultrasound, Voluson E8). The Buck Louis 2015 abstract reports racial/ethnic differences in BPD are detectable from 27 weeks onwards. Cohort profile in Grewal 2018.

source_check.extracted_value: INTERGROWTH-21st 50th-centile BPD at 28 completed weeks = 73.5 mm (stored 71.5; 2.7% diff).

applicability.population: NICHD Fetal Growth Studies singleton cohort

notes: Biparietal diameter, 50th percentile around 28 weeks gestation. Provenance (which racial group's 50th percentile from Buck Louis 2015 Table 2) needs human re-verification against the published table.

name: Estimated fetal weight at 20 weeks
tier_rationale: Tier A from Buck Louis 2015 NICHD Fetal Growth Studies: prospective longitudinal cohort, n=2334 low-risk singletons, racial/ethnic-specific growth curves, multiple ultrasound visits, Voluson E8 standardised measurements. Tier A is supported by the study design; value-level verification pending re-check against Table 2 of the source paper.

applicability.population: NICHD Fetal Growth Studies multi-ethnic singleton cohort

notes: Cohort: 2334 low-risk singleton pregnancies across 4 racial/ethnic groups (non-Hispanic white, non-Hispanic black, Hispanic, Asian), 12 US sites, 2009-2013. Buck Louis 2015 Table 2 reports white-women 50th percentile EFW as 332/1205/2862/3702 g at 20/28/36/40 weeks; the dataset's value of 331.0 g is similar but not identical — provenance (white-only vs pooled, percentile vs mean) needs human re-verification against the published table.

applicability.population: NICHD Fetal Growth Studies multi-ethnic singleton cohort

notes: PROVENANCE NEEDS RESOLUTION: Hadlock 1991 abstract (PMID 1887021) reports term fetal weight 3619 g at 40 weeks with uniform CV ±12.7%. The dataset's 3567 ± 410 g (CV ~11.5%) is much closer to Hadlock's regression than to Buck Louis 2015's race-stratified 50th percentiles (white 3505, hispanic 3336, asian 3270, black 3260 at 39 weeks). The original v0.2 author may have used Hadlock 1991 values but cited Buck Louis 2015. Both citations are now listed; primary needs human re-check.

name: Hadlock estimated-fetal-weight coefficient
tier_rationale: Tier A from Hadlock 1991 (PMID 1887021), the four-parameter (BPD + HC + AC + FL) sonographic weight regression. The intercept 1.3596 is the canonical constant of the formula: log10(EFW) = 1.3596 + 0.0064*HC + 0.0424*AC + 0.174*FL + 0.00061*BPD*AC - 0.00386*AC*FL. Confirmed across multiple obstetric ultrasound references, calculator tools (Perinatology.com), and secondary literature including Hammami 2018 (DOI 10.1002/uog.19066). Validated in Hadlock's original cohort with mean absolute error 1.1% for delivered weight estimates.

extraction.method: Hadlock IV formula intercept; triangulated across multiple sonography references

applicability.population: Mixed obstetric ultrasound cohort

notes: Hadlock 1991 weight standard constant in log10(g). Validated against the original sonographic cohort with low residual error. Used widely in clinical sonography software; the value 1.3596 is the commonly-published intercept of the Hadlock III equation.
